title: Insomnia Severity Self-Check
description: Based on the Insomnia Severity Index (ISI), this self-check helps you understand the current severity of your sleep difficulties.
instructions: For each question below, rate how much each has been a problem for you over the past 2 weeks.

questions.text: Waking up too early in the morning
How satisfied/dissatisfied are you with your current sleep pattern?
How noticeable to others do you think your sleep problem is (in terms of impairing your quality of life)?
How worried/distressed are you about your current sleep problem?
How much does your sleep problem interfere with your daily functioning (fatigue, mood, concentration, memory, ability to function at work/daily tasks)?

bands.label: No Clinically Significant Insomnia
bands.description: Your responses suggest minimal or no insomnia. Continue practicing good sleep habits to maintain healthy sleep.

bands.label: Subthreshold Insomnia
bands.description: Your responses suggest mild sleep difficulties. The strategies in this course — especially sleep scheduling and stimulus control — can help improve your sleep. Monitor your patterns and implement changes gradually.

bands.label: Moderate Insomnia
bands.description: Your responses suggest moderate insomnia that is likely affecting your daily functioning. The techniques in this course can help significantly. Consider also consulting a healthcare provider, especially if symptoms have persisted for more than 3 months.

bands.label: Severe Insomnia
bands.description: Your responses suggest severe insomnia. While this course provides helpful strategies, please consult a healthcare provider for a comprehensive evaluation. Professional CBT-I treatment is strongly recommended for severe insomnia.

disclaimer: This is an educational self-check tool based on the Insomnia Severity Index. It is NOT a clinical diagnosis. For accurate diagnosis and treatment, please consult a healthcare provider or sleep specialist.
